zh.lifeSummary: 當Urraca Alfonso de León於大約1197年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，她的父親Alfonso IX de León27歲，母親Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza65歲。她於1214年與Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya在西班牙結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1250年在西班牙安達盧希亞享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Urraca Alfonso de León于大约1197年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，她的父亲Alfonso IX de León27岁，母亲Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza65岁。她于1214年与Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya在西班牙结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1250年在西班牙安達盧希亞享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Carlos, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon, Luis, Rafael, Jesus, Gustavo.  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Elio, Heriberto, Leonardo, Lorenzo, Aldo, Angelo, Annamaria, Antonino, Carmelo, Carmine.
Spanish and southern Italian: from the personal name Alfonso, derived from the Visigothic name Adefonsus, formed from Gothic hathu ‘war’ + funs ‘ready’. Compare Alphonso .

variants.name: Alfonsi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ferdinando, Antonio, Benigno, Cesare, Elisabetta, Giuseppe, Italo, Marco, Mario, Mirella, Silvio, Tiberio.
Italian (southern): patronymic or plural form of   Alfonso  .

variants.name: D'Alonzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Augustino, Domenic, Emelio, Fiore, Rocco, Romeo.
Italian (D'Alonzo): patronymic from Alonso, avariant of the personal name Alfonso .

variants.name: De Fronzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Rocco, Angelo, Biagio, Saverio.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Fronzo, a shortform of Alfonso .

variants.name: Alphonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Andres, Belmiro, Eulalia, Gaspar, Manuel.  French Celestine, Marcel, Pierre.
Altered form of Spanish and southern Italian   Alfonso  .

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Jorge, Francisco, Pedro, Luis, Roberto, Angel, Enrique, Jesus, Antonio, Leonardo, Cecilio, Dario, Marco, Angelo, Clemente, Elio, Heriberto, Ciriaco, Delio.
Spanish: from the personal name Alonso, the Castilian variant of Alfonso .

variants.name: Di Fonzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Alfonso, Biagio, Carmine, Domenic, Eliseo, Eraldo, Onofrio, Panfilo, Pio, Raffaele, Renato, Salvatore, Valentino.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Fonzo, a shortform of Alfonso . The change of s to z is typicalof southern and central Italy.

variants.name: Afonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Antonio, Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Domingos, Ana, Mario, Abilio, Alfredo, Americo, Arlindo, Francisco, Sergio.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Caetano, Lidio.
Portuguese: from the personal name Afonso, an equivalent of Spanish Alfonso .

variants.name: Alphonse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Marcel, Raymonde, Antoine, Benoit, Clovis, Hermite, Jacques, Michel, Pierre, Ricot, Yves.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Seychellois: from the French personal name Alphonse, from Alphonsus, a Latinized spelling of Spanish Alfonso .

variants.name: Alfonzo
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Alfonso  .

variants.name: D'Alfonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Alfonso, Claudio, Ermanno, Sal, Angelo, Carmel, Delio, Domenic, Domenico, Fiore, Mauro, Nunzi, Nunzio, Pasquale.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name   Alfonso  ; thisform is typical of Abruzzo.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Urraca Alfonso de León was born about 1197, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, her father, Alfonso IX de León, was 27 and her mother, Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza, was 65. She married Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya in 1214, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. She died about 1250, in Andalusia, Spain, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Urraca Alfonso de León est née environ 1197, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Alfonso IX de León, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza, avait 65 ans. Elle a épousé Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya en 1214, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1250, à Andalucía, Espagne, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Urraca Alfonso de León um 1197 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alfonso IX de León 27 und ihre Mutter Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza 65. Sie hat 1214 in Spanien Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1250 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Andalucía, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Urraca Alfonso de León è nata il circa 1197 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, sua padre, Alfonso IX de León, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza, aveva 65 anni. Lei sposato Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya nel 1214, nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì circa 1250, nel Andalucía, Spagna, all'età di 55 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Urraca Alfonso de León(이)가 대략 1197년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfonso IX de León(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza(은)는 65세였다. 그녀는 1214년 스페인에서 Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1250년 스페인 앤다루ci아에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Urraca Alfonso de León nasceu aproximadamente 1197, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso IX de León, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza, tinha 65 anos. Ela casou-se com Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya em 1214, em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1250, em Andaluzia, Espanha, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Urraca Alfonso de León родилась около 1197 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, её отцу по имени Alfonso IX de León было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza, 65. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya в 1214 г. в местности Испания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла около 1250 г. в местности андалукiа, Испания в возрасте 55 лет.

Se casó con Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya en 1214, en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1250, en Andalucía, España, a la edad de 55 años.
